message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): Arc<DistributedHttpClient> shared across tasks (tick 365)

Added `distributed_http_client_shared_arc_across_tasks` — runtime
companion to the tick-364 compile-time Send+Sync assertion.

Tick 364 proves the trait bounds hold at compile time. Tick 365
exercises them: ONE shared `Arc<DistributedHttpClient>` is cloned
(Arc refcount-increment) into 3 concurrent request tasks, each
fires a GET, all three must land on the server + succeed.

Why both: compile-time assertion proves the type is usable with Arc;
runtime test proves the inner connection pool doesn't secretly assume
single-threaded access (no panics on concurrent pool access, no
shared-state corruption).

Post-assertion sanity: Arc::strong_count returns to 1 after joining
all handles — proves no task leaked a client clone.

The pattern tested here IS the production pattern for distributed RPC
code: one Arc stored in the orchestrator, cheaply cloned into each
outbound task. If a future refactor broke connection-pool
thread-safety (e.g. by switching from Mutex to RefCell), this test
would fail at runtime instead of a silent production race.

Complements:
- tick 318 distributed_http_client_concurrent_gets (per-task clients)
- tick 364 distributed_http_client_is_send_and_sync (compile-time)
- This tick (shared Arc, runtime)

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 17s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
